sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.27 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: a) Let$t$be an involution of a finite group$G$and suppose that the set$D=t^G \{ t^xt^y x,y G, \; \; |t^xt^y|=2\}$does not intersect$O_2(G)$. Prove that if$t O_2(C(d))$for any involution$d$from$C_D(t)$, then$D=t^G$(and in this case the structure of the group$< D >$is known). b) A significantly more general question. Let$t$be an involution of a finite group$G$and suppose that$t Z ^{ }(N(X))$for ever...
